label: Healthy
description: Leaf color distribution looks balanced and there are no strong stress signals.
treatment: Continue regular watering, nutrient balance, and field monitoring.

label: Leaf Blight
description: Brown or scorched regions often point to fungal leaf blight stress.
treatment: Remove badly infected leaves, avoid overhead watering, and apply a suitable bio-fungicide.

label: Early Blight
description: Dry brown lesions and patchy necrosis commonly match early blight-like stress on solanaceous crops.
treatment: Remove affected lower leaves, improve ventilation, and follow a fungicide plan recommended for blight-prone crops.

label: Nitrogen Deficiency
description: Yellowing patterns can indicate nutrient deficiency, especially low nitrogen.
treatment: Use a balanced nitrogen-rich fertilizer and review soil health.

label: Rust Infection
description: Orange-brown tones may suggest rust-like fungal damage on the leaf surface.
treatment: Improve airflow, remove affected leaves, and use a recommended rust-control fungicide.
